title: worker2 won't start even after you'd swear you fixed it
description_md:
The `worker2` background service is down. `systemctl start worker2` reports `start request repeated too quickly` and refuses outright -- it doesn't even try. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), find out what's actually wrong with the service itself, fix that, and then get systemd to actually let it start again.

You're done when `systemctl is-active worker2` reports `active`.
